evidence_slots.subject: software executing CSR operations

evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Read logic directly returns DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH, supervisor, and machine CSRs when csr_read selects the address. Write logic under csr_we directly updates DCSR and later machine CSRs such as MSTATUS/MEDELEG/MIDELEG/MIE/MTVEC. The provided search found no occurrences of csr_addr.priv/read_only/privilege access gating. assign priv_lvl_o = (debug_mode_q || umode_i) ? M : priv_lvl_q also elevates when umode_i is set. Illegal instruction generation is tied to update_access_exception/read_access_exception, but observed decode generally does not set these for privilege violations.

evidence_slots.impact: If software in lower privilege can reach csr_regfile without an external decoder blocking it, it can read/write machine/debug/supervisor CSRs, alter trap/debug/interrupt/cache policy, or expose dpc/dscratch state.

inspection_records.notes: Missing impact slot filled from security consequence of changing VM/memory policy.

evidence_slots.subject: debug/JTAG reset controller and DMI requester
evidence_slots.operation: reset, activate/deactivate debug module, clear debug state
evidence_slots.object: debug module active/reset state, DMI request/response state, SBA address/data registers, hart debug state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: External debug reset controls should be authorized and should not leave security-sensitive debug/SBA state active or stale after dmactive deassertion, DMI reset, non-debug reset, or ndmreset transitions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs clears debug control, command, abstractauto, progbuf, data, sbcs, sbaddr, and sbdata on power reset. When dmcontrol_q.dmactive is false, it synchronously clears haltreq/resumereq/hartreset/hartsel/ndmreset and command/data/SBA state while allowing only dmactive to be written. dmi_jtag resets JTAG-side dr/state/address/data/error on trst_ni and clears dr_d on test_logic_reset; pass is loaded from jtag_key on rst_ni reset.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG reset/DMI reset -> dmi_jtag TAP state; DMI DMControl.dmactive -> dm_csrs state clear -> dm_top/dm_sba/dm_mem affected state
evidence_slots.impact: Observed reset handling reduces stale debug/SBA state exposure on PoR and dmactive deassertion by clearing control/data/address registers; no stale-state vulnerability is supported by provided evidence.

rtl_evidence.snippet: On ~rst_ni: dmcontrol_q, command_q, abstractauto_q, progbuf_q, data_q, sbcs_q, sbaddr_q, sbdata_q cleared; havereset_q set.
If !dmcontrol_q.dmactive: clears haltreq/resumereq/hartreset/hartsel/ndmreset and command/data/SBA registers; only dmactive loaded from dmcontrol_d.dmactive.
test_logic_reset clears dr_d; trst_ni reset clears dr_q,state_q,address_q,data_q,error_q.
On ~rst_ni pass <= jtag_key.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: No source showing authorization for external reset controls; however, the specific stale-state clearing obligation is satisfied by observed reset assignments.
inspection_records.notes: Impact request addressed as no supported adverse impact for state residue based on available reset evidence.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO requester and external JTAG requester
evidence_slots.operation: configure/read/expose debug authentication key material or use key for pass check
evidence_slots.object: jtag_key, ROM2 secure_reg key entries, pkey/key-location related data controlling debug authentication
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG authentication key or key-location/configuration registers should be protected from unauthorized writes/reads and reset defaults should not enable debug access by default.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 initializes const mem with fixed key values including a JTAG entry and AES entry. secure_reg is reset-loaded from mem, but on req_i && we_i writes secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i; rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q]. ariane_peripherals exposes rom2_req/rom2_we/rom2_addr/rom2_wdata from axi2mem and has jtag_key output/key_reg_out signals. dmi_jtag samples jtag_key into pass on reset. No visible lock, privilege, read masking, or lifecycle guard protects ROM2 key storage.
evidence_slots.path: AXI/peripheral bus -> ariane_peripherals axi2rom2 -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata -> secure_reg/rdata/key_reg_out/jtag_key -> dmi_jtag pass/pass_chk
evidence_slots.state_condition: ~rst_ni loads keys; req_i && we_i writes selected secure_reg; !we_i read sets raddr_q; rdata_o returns secure_reg.
evidence_slots.impact: Software or bus masters may overwrite or read key material, allowing debug authentication bypass or secret disclosure; fixed reset constants further reduce key secrecy.

rtl_evidence.snippet: const mem contains fixed entries: access-control data, JTAG key, AES key.
On reset secure_reg <= mem; else if req_i and we_i secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i; if !we_i raddr_q <= addr index.
assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
ROM2_FUSE peripheral has rom2_req, rom2_addr, rom2_we, pkey_loc_o, key_reg_out, rom2_rdata, rom2_wdata.
axi2mem i_axi2rom2 connects slave rom2_fuse to req_o/we_o/addr_o.
On reset, pass <= jtag_key.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The exact ariane_peripherals assignment from key_reg_out to jtag_key was not fully shown; fact layer confirms jtag_key output and pkey/key signals.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: JTAG/AES key material is stored as reset-loaded ROM2 secure_reg but is writable through bus-facing rom2.we_i, readable through rdata_o, and used to populate jtag_key/pass without visible privilege/lock protection.
inspection_records.notes: This also supports ROM2 write-protection concern.

evidence_slots.subject: SoC bus/APB/AXI requester
evidence_slots.operation: write or overwrite ROM/key/boot data, or read ROM2 data
evidence_slots.object: ROM2 contents secure_reg and rdata_o/key-related material
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM2/boot/key material should be read-only or write-protected; any programming write should require privileged lifecycle/debug/programming authorization.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 has req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i. On reset secure_reg loads constant mem; after reset any req_i with we_i writes secure_reg at addr index with wdata_i. rdata_o returns secure_reg. ariane_peripherals wires ROM2 through axi2mem with rom2_we/rom2_wdata/rom2_addr. No guard beyond req_i/we_i or bounds check before the write is visible.
evidence_slots.path: AXI fabric/peripheral region -> ariane_peripherals i_axi2rom2 -> rom2_req/rom2_we/rom2_addr/rom2_wdata -> i_rom2 secure_reg/rdata_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: After reset, req_i=1 and we_i=1; address index selected by addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3].
evidence_slots.impact: A bus master can corrupt or replace key/boot/access-control data stored in ROM2, potentially enabling JTAG bypass, crypto key manipulation, or access-policy corruption.

rtl_evidence.snippet: rom2 ports include req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i, rdata_o, secure_reg.
mem contains key/access-control constants.
if(req_i) if (!we_i) raddr_q <= index; else secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i.
rdata_o exposes secure_reg[raddr_q].
axi2mem i_axi2rom2 produces rom2_req, rom2_we, rom2_addr from bus slave.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Complete address decode and bus privilege policy for rom2_fuse was truncated, but rom2.sv itself enforces no write protection.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: ROM2 is writable despite containing boot/key material and being named ROM/fuse; write enable from bus-facing axi2mem directly updates secure_reg without authorization or write protection.
inspection_records.notes: The write path is explicit and high-confidence.

evidence_slots.subject: software/APB requester
evidence_slots.object: AES/key-related APB registers and packet/security configuration signals aes_* and pkt_*
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Security-sensitive peripheral register banks should require privilege/range/lock checks and should mask secret key/intermediate/plaintext state on APB reads unless permitted.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_peripherals contains APB-to-reg bridge instances and AES/packet APB signals (aes_penable/aes_pwrite/aes_paddr/aes_psel/aes_pwdata/aes_prdata; pkt_*). Fact layer shows bridge instantiation locations, but target AES/packet register implementations and read/write muxes were not provided in the tool observations. No concrete secret read or unguarded write behavior can be confirmed from the visible evidence alone.
evidence_slots.path: AXI/APB bridge in ariane_peripherals -> i_apb_to_reg_aes and i_apb_to_reg_pkt -> target peripheral register signals/modules
evidence_slots.state_condition: APB psel/penable/pwrite and paddr select AES or packet regions; target behavior unknown.
evidence_slots.impact: Potential exposure or modification of AES keys/config/plaintext could compromise cryptographic confidentiality/integrity, but no specific RTL behavior proving that impact is available in the provided observations.

rtl_evidence.snippet: pkt_penable/pwrite/paddr/psel/pwdata/prdata/pready/pslverr signals exist.
aes_penable/pwrite/paddr/psel/pwdata/prdata/pready/pslverr signals exist.
i_apb_to_reg_pkt instance listed in fact layer.
i_apb_to_reg_aes instance listed in fact layer.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need APB decode around AES/pkt, target register implementation, read data muxes, lock/privilege checks, and whether key/intermediate registers are readable.
inspection_records.notes: Missing impact request addressed as potential only; no candidate violation asserted.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI bus master such as CPU, debug SBA master, or peripheral/DMA-like requester
evidence_slots.object: SoC address map regions reachable via AXI master/slave connects, including debug module, CLINT, boot ROM, peripherals, SPI/Ethernet bridges
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Fabric should preserve requester identity/security attributes and enforce address ranges so debug/DMA/external-facing controllers cannot access protected memory/MMIO outside authorized regions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Visible facts show debug AXI master/slave connections, SPI/Ethernet AXI clock converters/slaves, and awprot/arprot-like signals. dm_top connects dm_sba to axi_m_req_o and axi_slave_connect_rev/axi2mem for debug slave. ariane_peripherals has AXI/APB/peripheral bridge instances. However, connect module implementations, complete address decode constants, security attribute handling, and range enforcement were not included in observations.
evidence_slots.path: debug master/slave and peripheral AXI connections in dm_top/ariane_testharness/ariane_peripherals -> AXI fabric/connect modules -> memory/MMIO slaves
evidence_slots.state_condition: AXI aw/ar valid transactions from CPU/debug/peripheral masters; address decode/prot/user handling unknown.
evidence_slots.impact: If address decode or requester/security attributes are absent, debug or peripheral masters could reach protected memory/MMIO; provided evidence is insufficient to prove or disprove this fabric-level impact.

rtl_evidence.snippet: dm_sba drives axi_req_o (axi_m_req_o) and receives axi_resp_i.
axi_slave_connect_rev and axi2mem connect debug slave AXI to dm_mem req/we/addr/wdata/rdata.
SPI AXI slave signals include awaddr and awprot.
i_axi_master_dm and i_axi_slave_dm instances listed in fact layer.
CLINT and Ariane AXI connect instances listed in fact layer.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need ariane_soc_pkg address constants, AXI connect implementation/decode, awprot/arprot/user tie-offs or propagation, and any PMP/fabric policy enforcement.
inspection_records.notes: Missing impact request addressed as conditional; no violation asserted from incomplete fabric evidence.

14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };

25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;

30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.

33:         if (~rst_ni) begin

36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];

40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;

43:         end
